title: RNA concentration measurement and non-denaturing agarose gel electrophoresis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dkd0q","text":"RNA concentration measurement and non-denaturing agarose gel electrophoresis","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: •Determine RNA concentration using a NanoDropTM 2000/2000c Spectrophotometers (Nucleic Acids – RNA mode, 2 µL sample size)
•Determine RNA purity from protein contamination by assuring 260/280 ratio is 2.0

•Determine RNA integrity and purity from genomic DNA contamination by electrophoresis. Load 400 ng RNA onto a 1% agarose gel with ethidium bromide (EtBr) in TBE buffer and run at 70 V until the dye line is approximately 75-80% of the way down the gel.
•Visualize and document using a gel UV imaging/documentation system
creator: Lisa-Maria Rosenthal
